commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer product/security approval for an open implementation PR, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: The diff is security-sensitive but I found no concrete bypass: mutating/runtime tools remain default-denied, apply_patch is excluded, and allowlisted exec/process are tagged owner-only. (sha=19443a0df7703d58398a3e5e9aae004c76031066)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an unwrapped coding-tool factory for `/tools/invoke`, wires core coding primitives into the HTTP resolver, extends the default deny list for mutating tools, and updates gateway docs/tests/changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows `/tools/invoke` resolves only gateway/OpenClaw tools today, while the PR body gives before/after HTTP output for `read` and the default-denied mutating tools.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer product/security approval for an open implementation PR, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
## Summary

- `resolveGatewayScopedTools` only included `createOpenClawTools` (gateway management tools); `createOpenClawCodingTools` (exec, edit, read, browser, etc.) was never wired in
- The [official docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api#tools-invoke-api) describe `exec` as blocked by the HTTP deny list by default — implying the intent was always for coding tools to be available on this surface, gated by `gateway.tools.allow`
- This PR completes that wiring: coding tools are now reachable via `POST /tools/invoke`, subject to the existing deny list and policy pipeline
- Dedup: gateway tools take precedence over coding tools of the same name

## Root Cause

The deny list in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` already included `exec`, `spawn`, `apply_patch`, etc. — designed to gate exactly these tools on the HTTP surface. But `createOpenClawCodingTools` was never passed to `resolveGatewayScopedTools`, so the deny list had nothing to gate: coding tools simply weren't present at all. The docs described the intended behavior; the implementation was incomplete.

## What did NOT change

- Security posture: `exec` and other high-risk tools remain on `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` — blocked by default, require explicit `gateway.tools.allow` to enable
- Agent session runners: no change to how tools are wired for LLM agent runs
- Tool policy pipeline: coding tools pass through the same `applyToolPolicyPipeline` + deny-list as all other tools

## Regression Test Plan

- Added `createOpenClawCodingTools` stub to the `vi.mock("../agents/pi-tools.js")` block in `tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — without this, all 22 existing tests failed with 500s (undefined function crash)
- Added new test: `exposes coding tools via /tools/invoke when agent policy allows them` — verifies a coding tool passes through policy + deny-list filtering and is callable via the HTTP surface

```
Tests  24 passed (24)  src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Before this PR, `POST /tools/invoke` did not expose coding-primitive tools (`read`/`write`/`edit`/`exec`/`process`/`apply_patch`). The PR wires `read` into the HTTP surface via a new unwrapped factory `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw()` (commit 1) and adds `write`/`edit`/`process` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` + excludes provider-gated `apply_patch` (commit 2). Two follow-up clawsweeper findings were addressed: **[P1]** HTTP-surface `exec`/`process` (when allowlisted via `gateway.tools.allow`) must require owner semantics, since the global owner-only fallback set only covers `cron`/`gateway`/`nodes`; the resolver now tags them `ownerOnly: true` post-construction (HTTP path only). **[P2]** the `disablePluginTools` flag was passed to the construction plan but ignored; the default plan now honors it and suppresses channel/OpenClaw/plugin tools end-to-end.
- **Real environment tested:** Docker container built fresh from this PR's latest head — `feat/tools-invoke-coding-tools-upstream` at **`19443a0df7`** (includes commits 1-2 + P1 fix + P2 fix + lint fix). Build performed inside a container based on `openclaw:main` with the PR source mounted at `/workspace`; `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` then `pnpm build:docker` produced a 92 MB `dist/`. Two kinds of proof exercised:
  - **Live HTTP gateway** (`node /workspace/openclaw.mjs gateway run` on port 19101) — full six-test suite for the visible HTTP behavior.
  - **Direct in-process Node calls** into the compiled `dist/tool-resolution-*.js` and `dist/pi-tools-*.js` (same bundles the running gateway loads) for the P1 ownerOnly tag and P2 raw-factory tool set — these expose internal state that the HTTP black-box can't directly observe.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# (1) Inside the container with PR source at /workspace, build
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build:docker

# (2) HTTP regression suite — start gateway, run six curls
node /workspace/openclaw.mjs gateway run &
for tool in read write edit process apply_patch; do
  curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19101/tools/invoke \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"tool":"'$tool'","args":{...},"sessionKey":"main"}'
done

# (3) Opt-in via gateway.tools.allow=["write"], restart, retry write

# (4) In-process verification of the P1 and P2 fixes
node /tmp/live-proof.mjs    # imports dist/tool-resolution-*.js, dist/pi-tools-*.js
```

- **Evidence after fix (all captured against rebuilt 19443a0df7):**

**(i) HTTP regression — six-test suite:**

```text
--- 1. READ via /tools/invoke (commit 1 — newly wired) ---
{"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello v3 at 2026-05-18T02:37:36Z
"}]}}
HTTP 200

--- 2. WRITE via /tools/invoke (commit 2 — default-denied) ---
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
HTTP 404

--- 3. EDIT via /tools/invoke (commit 2) ---
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
HTTP 404

--- 4. PROCESS via /tools/invoke (commit 2) ---
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: process"}}
HTTP 404

--- 5. APPLY_PATCH via /tools/invoke (commit 2 — excluded as provider-gated) ---
{"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
HTTP 404

--- 6. WRITE with gateway.tools.allow=["write"] opt-in ---
{"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 15 bytes to /tmp/opt-in-success-v3.txt"}]}}
HTTP 200
$ cat /tmp/opt-in-success-v3.txt
opt-in v3 works
```

**(ii) P1 fix — exec/process tagged ownerOnly on HTTP surface (direct call into compiled `dist/tool-resolution-*.js`):**

```text
[P1 HTTP surface with gateway.tools.allow=[exec,process]] (surface=http)
  tool count: 23
  read: present (ownerOnly=false)
  exec: present (ownerOnly=true)     ← P1 FIX
  process: present (ownerOnly=true)  ← P1 FIX

[Control: loopback surface (raw factory not applied)] (surface=loopback)
  tool count: 25
  read: absent
  exec: absent
  process: absent
```

The HTTP-only tagging does not bleed to the loopback surface (loopback doesn't receive raw codingTools at all per the `surface === "http"` guard).

**(iii) P2 fix — raw factory returns only the five coding primitives (direct call into compiled `dist/pi-tools-*.js`):**

```text
$ node live-proof-v2.mjs
===== PR #63919 P2 — disablePluginTools honored in raw factory =====

Tools returned by createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw (count: 5):
  - edit
  - exec
  - process
  - read
  - write

--- Assertions ---
  ✅ P0 read present (core coding primitive)
  ✅ P2 NO channel-only tools (found: [])
  ✅ P2 NO plugin-capable tools (found: [])
```

Before the P2 fix, the default `toolConstructionPlan` ignored `options?.disablePluginTools`, so `includeChannelTools` / `includeOpenClawTools` / `includePluginTools` remained true and the raw factory would have included `message`/`browser`/`web-search`/etc. After: exactly five core coding primitives, nothing else.

**(iv) Bundle-presence sanity:**

```text
$ grep -h "HTTP_OWNER_ONLY_CODING_TOOLS" /workspace/dist/*.js
const HTTP_OWNER_ONLY_CODING_TOOLS = new Set(["exec", "process"]);
for (const tool of rawCodingTools) if (HTTP_OWNER_ONLY_CODING_TOOLS.has(tool.name)) tool.ownerOnly = true;

$ grep -h "pluginToolsDisabled\|disablePluginTools === true" /workspace/dist/*.js
const pluginToolsDisabled = options?.disablePluginTools === true;
    includeChannelTools: includeCoreTools && !pluginToolsDisabled,
    includeOpenClawTools: includeCoreTools && !pluginToolsDisabled,
    includePluginTools: !pluginToolsDisabled
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The full PR scope is validated on the latest head:
  - `read` reachable via `/tools/invoke` (HTTP 200 + content) — commit 1.
  - `write`/`edit`/`process`/`apply_patch` all 404 by default — commit 2 deny-list.
  - `gateway.tools.allow=["write"]` opt-in unblocks write (HTTP 200 + file written) — commit 2 opt-in path.
  - `exec`/`process` carry `ownerOnly: true` on the HTTP surface when allowlisted — P1 fix.
  - The raw factory returns exactly five coding primitives, no channel/plugin tools — P2 fix.
  - The HTTP-only `ownerOnly` tagging does NOT bleed into the loopback surface — scope contained.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end non-owner scope rejection over the wire (the gateway refuses to start `bind=lan` with `auth.mode=none`, so demonstrating the trusted-proxy operator.write-only path requires a layered reverse proxy — added complexity not warranted here since the in-process result directly confirms the `ownerOnly: true` tag is present, and `applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy` semantics are covered by existing tests in `tool-policy.ts`). Tested by unit tests: `tool-resolution.test.ts` (`marks HTTP-surface exec/process coding tools as ownerOnly`) and `pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts` (`honors disablePluginTools: true in the default construction plan`).
- **Before evidence:** Pre-PR (current upstream/main without commits 1-2): `curl POST /tools/invoke {"tool":"read",...}` returns `404 not_found` because `read` is not in `createOpenClawTools` and was not appended to the HTTP surface. Pre-P1 (commits 1-2 only, before P1 fix): `exec`/`process` returned from the resolver had `ownerOnly` unset, so a trusted-proxy caller with `operator.write` but no `operator.admin` could reach them once `gateway.tools.allow` lifted the deny. Pre-P2 (before this fix): the raw factory included channel/OpenClaw/plugin tools because the default construction plan ignored `disablePluginTools` — `message`/`browser`/etc. would have appeared in the in-process tool list shown above.

## Human Verification

- `npx vitest run src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — 24/24 pass on a clean branch based on `upstream/main`
- Pre-commit hooks (tsgo, oxlint, lint:auth) passed

## Relationship to existing PRs

- #55906 (`fix(gateway): deny nodes over HTTP tools invoke`) — orthogonal: restricts the surface; this PR expands it. Both coexist.
- #55724 (`fix(gateway): enforce owner-only tools in invoke API`) — orthogonal: enforces owner-only policy. Our change passes `senderIsOwner: true` for HTTP bearer auth, consistent with that direction.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** operator enables `exec` via `gateway.tools.allow` without understanding the RCE surface  
  **Mitigation:** `exec` is on `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` — explicit opt-in required; no behavior change for default installs
- **Risk:** dedup logic (gateway tools shadow coding tools of the same name) could suppress a tool unexpectedly  
  **Mitigation:** gateway and coding tool names are disjoint in practice; merge order is documented in the code comment

Addresses #37131
target.author: simonusa

[P1 HTTP surface with gateway.tools.allow=[exec,process]] (surface=http)

  - edit
  - exec
  - process
  - read
  - write

--- Assertions ---

  ✅ P2 NO plugin-capable tools (found: [])

for (const tool of rawCodingTools) if (HTTP_OWNER_ONLY_CODING_TOOLS.has(tool.name)) tool.ownerOnly = true;

    includeOpenClawTools: includeCoreTools && !pluginToolsDisabled,

- **Observed result after fix:** The full PR scope is validated on the latest head:
  - `read` reachable via `/tools/invoke` (HTTP 200 + content) — commit 1.
  - `write`/`edit`/`process`/`apply_patch` all 404 by default — commit 2 deny-list.
